finance-markets.title: Goldman bond traders take shine off bumper quarter
finance-markets.summary: Equities unit powers bank’s earnings, while fixed income, currencies and commodities business fell well short

finance-markets.title: Pope Leo says he has ‘no fear’ of Trump administration
finance-markets.summary: Comments from Catholic Church head come hours after US president launched scathing attack on him

finance-markets.title: Why time is on Iran’s side
finance-markets.summary: A global energy crisis is only just beginning. Political turmoil will follow

finance-markets.title: Iran threatens to hit back against Middle East ports ahead of US blockade
finance-markets.summary: Donald Trump tells US navy to ‘interdict’ ships that pay Iran to pass through Strait of Hormuz

finance-markets.title: EU ties €35bn fund release to Hungary’s break with Orbán era
finance-markets.summary: Brussels links unfreezing of funds to 27 conditions it expects incoming prime minister Péter Magyar to deliver on

finance-markets.title: Starmer defends fast-tracking EU rules into UK law
finance-markets.summary: Prime minister says mechanism would help cut business costs and bring down prices

finance-markets.title: Carmakers’ new weapon to tackle EV range anxiety
finance-markets.summary: VW, Renault and BMW consider range-extended vehicles to entice drivers not ready for full electric switch

finance-markets.title: Hungary’s Election Winner Is Not Seeking Changes to Central Bank
finance-markets.summary: Election winner Peter Magyar vowed to work with central bank Governor Mihaly Varga, saying Hungary needs to focus on difficult financial and economic situation right now.

finance-markets.title: US Says It Will Intercept, Divert or Capture Ships Leaving Iran
finance-markets.summary: The US fleshed out plans to blockade Iranian ports, publishing a notice that said it could capture ships that contravene the measures.

finance-markets.title: Goldman Shares Fall as Bond Trading Miss Outweighs Equity Record
finance-markets.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. shares fell after the bank reported a surprise drop in bond-trading revenue, casting a shadow over what was another record quarter from the firm’s equities team.

science-space.title: Hubble Spies an Active Spiral
science-space.summary: A luminous swirl set against the deep black of space, the barred spiral galaxy IC 486 glows with a soft, ethereal light in this new NASA Hubble Space Telescope image. IC 486 lies right on the edge of the constellation Gemini (the Twins), around 380 million light-years from Earth. Classified as a barred spiral galaxy, […]

science-space.title: NASA Science, Cargo Launch Aboard Northrop Grumman CRS-24
science-space.summary: NASA is sending more science, technology demonstrations, and crew supplies to the International Space Station following the successful launch of the agency’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24. Northrop Grumman’s Cygnus XL spacecraft, carrying approximately 11,000 pounds of cargo to the orbiting laboratory, lifted off at 7:41 a.m. EDT Saturday […]

science-space.title: NASA Welcomes Record-Setting Artemis II Moonfarers Back to Earth
science-space.summary: The first astronauts to travel to the Moon in more than half a century are back on Earth after a record-setting mission aboard NASA’s Artemis II test flight. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, and Christina Koch, and CSA (Canadian Space Agency) astronaut Jeremy Hansen splashed down at 5:07 p.m. PDT Friday off the coast […]

science-space.title: New Perspective of Home
science-space.summary: Seen during Artemis II’s lunar flyby on April 6, 2026, the Moon and Earth align in the same frame, each partially illuminated by the Sun. The Moon’s surface appears in sharp detail in the foreground, while Earth sits much farther away, smaller and softly lit in the background. A faint reflection in the spacecraft window […]

science-space.title: Earthset From the Lunar Far Side
science-space.summary: The crew of NASA’s Artemis II mission captured extraordinary images of our home planet during their journey around the far side of the Moon.

science-space.title: 'God of Chaos' asteroid Apophis will blaze across the sky on April 13, 2029 — here's why this once-in-a-lifetime event is worth traveling for
science-space.summary: A rare stargazing spectacle will unfold on Friday, April 13, 2029, as the asteroid Apophis passes closer than satellites over Europe and Africa in a true once-in-a-lifetime event.

science-space.title: A worst-case solar storm could knock out satellites, GPS and power grids, report warns
science-space.summary: Scientists outline how a once-in-a-century solar storm could disrupt the technology modern society depends on.

science-space.title: From spa to boat party: 10 epic events for the Aug. 12, 2026, total solar eclipse in Spain and Iceland
science-space.summary: Experience the Aug. 12, 2026 total solar eclipse from Spain and Iceland with festivals, spa sessions and skywatching events along the path of totality.

science-space.title: 'The most special thing that will ever happen in my life': Artemis 2 astronauts describe their epic moon mission
science-space.summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts are back on Earth, and they've begun processing their historic moon mission. But it's still tough for them to put the experience into words.

science-space.title: SpaceX launches huge 'Cygnus XL' cargo ship carrying over 5 tons of supplies to ISS astronauts (video)
science-space.summary: Northrop Grumman's second "Cygnus XL" cargo ship launched toward the International Space Station on Saturday morning (April 11).

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

cybersecurity.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
cybersecurity.summary: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

cybersecurity.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

cybersecurity.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
cybersecurity.summary: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

cybersecurity.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
cybersecurity.summary: Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic

cybersecurity.title: Your MTTD Looks Great. Your Post-Alert Gap Doesn't
cybersecurity.summary: Anthropic restricted its Mythos Preview model last week after it autonomously found and exploited zero-day vulnerabilities in every major operating system and browser. Palo Alto Networks' Wendi Whitmorewarned that similar capabilities are weeks or months from proliferation. CrowdStrike's 2026 Global Threat Report puts average eCrime breakout time at 29 minutes. Mandiant's M-Trends 2026

cybersecurity.title: North Korea's APT37 Uses Facebook Social Engineering to Deliver RokRAT Malware
cybersecurity.summary:
The North Korean hacking group tracked as APT37 (aka ScarCruft) has been attributed to a fresh multi-stage, social engineering campaign in which threat actors approached targets on Facebook and added them as friends on the social media platform, turning the trust-building exercise into a delivery channel for a remote access trojan called RokRAT.
"The threat actor used two Facebook

cybersecurity.title: OpenAI Revokes macOS App Certificate After Malicious Axios Supply Chain Incident
cybersecurity.summary:
OpenAI revealed a GitHub Actions workflow used to sign its macOS apps led to the download of the malicious Axios library on March 31, but noted that no user data or internal system was compromised.
"Out of an abundance of caution, we are taking steps to protect the process that certifies our macOS applications are legitimate OpenAI apps," OpenAI said in a post last week. "We found no

cybersecurity.title: Citizen Lab: Law Enforcement Used Webloc to Track 500 Million Devices via Ad Data
cybersecurity.summary:
Hungarian domestic intelligence, the national police in El Salvador, and several U.S. law enforcement and police departments have been attributed to the use of an advertising-based global geolocation surveillance system called Webloc.
The tool was developed by Israeli company Cobwebs Technologies and is now sold by its successor Penlink after the two firms merged in July 2023

cybersecurity.title: GlassWorm Campaign Uses Zig Dropper to Infect Multiple Developer IDEs
cybersecurity.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged yet another evolution of the ongoing GlassWorm campaign, which employs a new Zig dropper that's designed to stealthily infect all integrated development environments (IDEs) on a developer's machine.
The technique has been discovered in an Open VSX extension named "specstudio.code-wakatime-activity-tracker," which masquerades as WakaTime, a

tech-ai.title: Slate Auto raises $650M to fund its affordable EV truck plans
tech-ai.summary: Slate Auto's latest funding round was led by existing investor TWG Global, a firm run by LA Dodgers owner Mark Walter.

tech-ai.title: Roblox introduces ‘Kids’ and ‘Select’ accounts for age-appropriate access to games and chat
tech-ai.summary: Users aged five to nine will be assigned to a "Roblox Kids" account, and users aged nine to 15 to will be put in a "Roblox Select" account.

tech-ai.title: From LLMs to hallucinations, here’s a simple guide to common AI terms
tech-ai.summary: The rise of AI has brought an avalanche of new terms and slang. Here is a glossary with definitions of some of the most important words and phrases you might encounter.

tech-ai.title: Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly building an AI clone to replace him in meetings
tech-ai.summary:
Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg could soon have an AI clone of himself to interact with and provide feedback to employees, according to a report from the Financial Times. Sources tell the outlet that Meta is training the AI avatar on Zuckerberg's image and voice, along with his mannerisms, tone, and public statements, "so that employees might feel more connected to the founder through interactions with it."
Meta may start allowing creators to make AI avatars of themselves if the experiment with Zuckerb

tech-ai.title: The Strait of Hormuz blockade is causing a slow-moving food crisis
tech-ai.summary:
A view of the vessels passing through Strait of Hormuz following the two-week temporary ceasefire reached between the United States and Iran, seen in Oman on April 8, 2026. | Shady Alassar/Anadolu via Getty Images	

Farmers are very busy in the spring, under pressure to get crops into the ground just as the Northern Hemisphere begins to thaw. But this year has been different for many, thanks in large part to the escalating war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.
The Strait of Hormuz

tech-ai.title: Roblox will need age verification to make sure you’re at least 9 years old
tech-ai.summary:
Roblox is about to make a big change to how you can access games on its platform: If you want to play any game that's intended for users nine or older, you will have to complete an age verification process. The shift is happening as part of a rollout of new Roblox Kids and Roblox Select accounts, which have different levels of protections for kids of different age groups.
Roblox has come under intense scrutiny over alleged child safety issues on the platform, and the company is the subject of mu

tech-ai.title: Roblox has games again
tech-ai.summary:
Roblox is making a subtle but notable change. After swapping the word "game" for "experience" all over its website and mobile apps in 2021 while the Epic Games v. Apple trial was taking place, Roblox is getting more comfortable with using the term "games" once again.
"Over time, we've learned that 'experiences' isn't an intuitive term for many parents, older players, and game developers," Roblox spokesperson Juliet Chaitin-Lefcourt tells The Verge. "'Games' is clearer and better reflects what Ro

tech-ai.title: Did Neuralink make the wrong bet?
tech-ai.summary:
Elon Musk promised Neuralink would bring superhuman abilities and minds merged with AI. Then he fueled a runaway hype train for his brain implant technology, which ended up with a grisly record for implants in monkeys and some success with human subjects. But for all of the hype, he's still further away than Mars from his goal. And that's because his relentless ambition is once again hitting the wall of scientific reality.
The heart of the issue is how brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) translate

tech-ai.title: Microsoft’s new Xbox Gamepad Cursor adds a virtual mouse to handhelds
tech-ai.summary:
Microsoft has started testing its own virtual mouse cursor inside its Xbox mode for Windows-based handhelds. The new Gamepad Cursor option provides a quick way to turn the left stick of a handheld like the Xbox Ally X into a virtual mouse.
While Asus has its own cursor as part of Armory Crate, Microsoft's Gamepad Cursor lives inside its own Xbox mode in Windows 11, and it's easy to activate from the Game Bar without having to use Armory Crate. Once activated, the Gamepad Cursor will turn your le

tech-ai.title: Sam Altman reportedly targeted in second attack
tech-ai.summary:
Sam Altman was seemingly targeted by a second attack on Sunday morning, with two suspects arrested following a shooting at his Russian Hill residence, The San Francisco Standard reports. The suspects were arrested and charged with negligent discharge, according to a police report on the incident, citing surveillance footage that appears to show a vehicle passenger firing a weapon at Altman's home.
This follows an earlier attack on Friday, in which a 20-year-old man was arrested for allegedly thr

world-news.title: Katya Adler: Jubilation in Budapest will be felt in Europe but leaves Moscow cold
world-news.summary: European leaders share Hungarians' joy over the ousting of Vladimir Putin's EU ally, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

world-news.title: Pope says he has 'no fear' of Trump after scathing criticism
world-news.summary: The US president accuses Leo of being "weak on crime and terrible for foreign policy".

world-news.title: Britney Spears goes into rehab after driving under the influence arrest
world-news.summary: The singer voluntarily checks herself into a treatment facility, three weeks before her court date.

world-news.title: Ghanaian footballer killed after armed attackers open fire at team bus
world-news.summary: The team was returning from a match when they were attacked by "masked men wielding guns", officials say.

world-news.title: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
world-news.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.

world-news.title: Carney on verge of Liberal majority government as votes cast in three by-elections
world-news.summary: One more seat could solidify the Canadian PM's hold on power after several Conservatives defected.

world-news.title: Iran carried out highest number of executions in decades
world-news.summary: Organisations warn many more people could be executed in the wake of the anti-government protests which swept Iran earlier this year.

world-news.title: The Dutch village at risk of being demolished
world-news.summary: Moerdijk has been earmarked for removal, to make way for a vast electricity substation.

world-news.title: Mourners sing Asha Bhosle hit as iconic Indian singer cremated
world-news.summary: The singer, who died at the age of 92, will be cremated with full state honours on Monday.

world-news.title: Israeli forces kill three Palestinians in Gaza, arrest dozens in West Bank
world-news.summary: Attacks come as Palestinian groups meet mediators from Egypt, Turkiye and Qatar in Cairo to discuss fragile 'ceasefire'.

health-biotech.title: Revolution Medicines touts 'unprecedented' data for pancreatic cancer pill
health-biotech.summary: Revolution Medicines reported that metastatic pancreatic cancer patients receiving its experimental KRAS inhibitor daraxonrasib achieved nearly double the survival time compared to those on chemotherapy in a Phase 3 trial. The company is preparing to seek FDA approval for the therapy, representing a significant advance in treatment options for this aggressive cancer.

health-biotech.title: Revolution Medicines' pancreatic cancer drug doubles survival time in Phase 3
health-biotech.summary: Revolution Medicines' experimental KRAS inhibitor daraxonrasib succeeded in a registrational Phase 3 trial for metastatic pancreatic cancer, demonstrating doubled survival compared to standard chemotherapy. The company plans to pursue FDA approval, marking a potential breakthrough for a disease with historically poor outcomes.

health-biotech.title: Allogene Therapeutics' CAR-T treatment eliminates residual cancer cells in B-cell lymphoma patients
health-biotech.summary: Allogene Therapeutics presented data demonstrating that its off-the-shelf CAR-T cell therapy effectively eliminated residual cancer cells in B-cell lymphoma patients. The results support the company's strategy to develop an easily administered cell therapy that could prevent cancer recurrence and overcome manufacturing delays associated with personalized CAR-T approaches.

health-biotech.title: Ideaya's uveal melanoma drug exceeds success benchmark in late-stage trial
health-biotech.summary: Ideaya Biosciences' investigational drug for uveal melanoma, a rare eye cancer, surpassed the company's predefined success criteria in a Phase 2/3 trial. The positive data strengthens the company's case for an accelerated FDA filing and potential expedited approval pathway.

health-biotech.title: Spyre Therapeutics IBD drug shows promise in early trial
health-biotech.summary: Spyre Therapeutics' inflammatory bowel disease treatment demonstrated positive efficacy in its first major clinical trial, positioning the company to compete with established pharmaceutical players in the IBD market. The success advances the biotech's development pipeline for this significant therapeutic indication.

health-biotech.title: China biotech's CRISPR therapy suggests US drugmakers will face competition
health-biotech.summary: A Chinese biotech company's CRISPR-based gene therapy for beta thalassemia has enabled five patients to discontinue regular blood transfusions, with the treatment potentially offering cost and safety advantages over existing therapies. The development signals emerging competitive pressure from international biotechs in advanced genetic medicine.

health-biotech.title: GSK advancing ovarian cancer drug mo-rez
health-biotech.summary: GlaxoSmithKline is progressing development of mo-rez, a novel ovarian cancer therapeutic, as part of an active biotech pipeline focused on oncology indications. The advancement reflects continued industry investment in addressing unmet needs in gynecologic malignancies.

health-biotech.title: Chasing a tough-to-treat lung disease, Avalyn plans an IPO
health-biotech.summary: Avalyn Pharmaceuticals, backed by Novo Holdings and other prominent investors, plans an initial public offering to fund development of improved therapies for idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis. The biotech positions its candidates as superior alternatives to existing treatments for this progressive and difficult-to-treat lung disease.

health-biotech.title: Invivyd reveals plans to test an antibody drug against measles
health-biotech.summary: Invivyd announced development of a monoclonal antibody therapeutic targeting measles, positioning it as a treatment option and alternative for vaccine-hesitant populations amid rising measles cases in the United States. The initiative addresses a resurgent public health concern while advancing immunotherapy applications.

health-biotech.title: Women have awaited a revolution in menopause. It hasn't arrived.
health-biotech.summary: Despite significant unmet medical need, drug development for menopause symptom management remains limited due to persistent systemic barriers in clinical research and regulatory pathways. The article examines obstacles preventing pharmaceutical innovation in addressing the diverse symptoms experienced by millions of women in midlife transition.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Despite European growth, VW reports global decline in BEV deliveries
automotive-ev-battery.summary: The Volkswagen Group delivered approximately 200,000 battery-electric vehicles in Q1 2026, representing an eight percent year-on-year decline despite strong double-digit growth in Europe. Sharp declines in China and the United States offset European gains, signaling challenging market dynamics for the world's largest automaker.

automotive-ev-battery.title: US battery recycler Ascend Elements files for insolvency
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Battery recycling startup Ascend Elements has filed for creditor protection following the Trump administration's decision to cut $316 million in federal funding for its Kentucky facility last fall. The company will undergo a restructuring process, highlighting challenges in the domestic battery recycling sector.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Volvo CE starts series production of electric articulated dump trucks
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Volvo Construction Equipment has commenced series production of its A30 Electric and A40 Electric articulated dump trucks at its Braås, Sweden facility, marking the first time electric dump trucks of this size have entered serial production. This milestone represents a significant shift in heavy-duty vehicle electrification.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Polestar reports over 13,000 EV deliveries in Q1
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Premium EV manufacturer Polestar delivered 13,126 electric vehicles in the first quarter of 2026, its strongest Q1 performance to date with a seven percent year-on-year increase. The results demonstrate sustained momentum for the Chinese-Swedish automaker in the competitive EV market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Tesla secures first European approval for 'FSD (Supervised)' driver assistance system
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Tesla has obtained regulatory approval from Dutch authority RDW for its Full Self-Driving (Supervised) system, marking the first European approval for the advanced driver assistance technology. Over-the-air software updates will begin deploying the system to customer vehicles immediately, with additional approvals being sought across Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: XCharge sets up European plant in Spain
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Charging infrastructure manufacturer XCharge has inaugurated its first European assembly plant in Silla, Valencia, Spain. The facility will begin assembling high-power chargers and battery systems for European customers starting in 2027, supporting regional EV charging expansion.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hyundai brings Ioniq line-up to China
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai has introduced its full electric Ioniq lineup to the Chinese market for the first time, including models from the Ioniq 5 to Ioniq 9, alongside two design concept vehicles ahead of Auto China 2026. The move addresses Hyundai's previous lag in the world's largest EV market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Austrian startup pushes for shared depot charging infrastructure
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Vienna-based mobility startup kW-Solutions has launched 'Charge with Friends', a platform enabling transport operators to share their charging infrastructure with peers to improve utilization rates. The initiative aims to enhance the economic viability of depot charging for commercial fleet operators.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Workhorse slashes prices by $60,000 and Purolater buys 100
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Commercial EV manufacturer Workhorse has reduced prices on its W56 step van by approximately $60,000, prompting a 100-unit order from courier service Purolater. The price reduction reflects competitive pressures in the commercial electric vehicle segment as operators accelerate fleet electrification.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Meyer Werft unveils concept for fully electric cruise ship
automotive-ev-battery.summary: German shipbuilder Meyer Werft has presented its 'Vision' project concept for what would be the world's first fully electric cruise ship with a gross tonnage exceeding 82,000 tons. The concept represents a significant effort to decarbonize the maritime industry.

crypto-web3.title: AI agents are set to power crypto payments, but a hidden flaw could expose wallets
crypto-web3.summary: Security researchers have identified a critical vulnerability in the AI infrastructure layer supporting crypto payments, where invisible systems can intercept sensitive data and steal credentials. The flaw has already been linked to stolen credentials and a $500,000 wallet drain, raising serious concerns about the security of AI-powered cryptocurrency transactions.

crypto-web3.title: Attacker exploits Polkadot-based Hyperbridge to mint 1 billion bridged DOT, nets $237K
crypto-web3.summary: A security breach in the Hyperbridge cross-chain bridge protocol allowed an attacker to mint 1 billion fraudulent Polkadot tokens, resulting in a $237,000 theft. Major exchanges Upbit and Bithumb subsequently suspended DOT deposits and withdrawals in response to the incident.

crypto-web3.title: 'Think bigger': Michael Saylor's Strategy buys another 13,927 bitcoin for $1 billion as holdings reach 780,897 BTC
crypto-web3.summary: MicroStrategy's continued Bitcoin accumulation strategy has brought its total holdings to 780,897 BTC, representing over 3.7% of the global Bitcoin supply valued at approximately $55 billion. The company's aggressive purchasing through its STRC preferred stock demonstrates sustained institutional confidence in Bitcoin as a long-term store of value.

crypto-web3.title: Crypto funds pull in $1.1 billion as bitcoin products lead strongest inflow week since January: CoinShares
crypto-web3.summary: Cryptocurrency investment products experienced their strongest weekly inflows since January, pulling in $1.1 billion as Bitcoin products led the surge. The influx was attributed to easing inflation concerns and reduced geopolitical tensions, signaling renewed institutional and retail interest in digital assets.

crypto-web3.title: Bitmine's Tom Lee calls ether 'the wartime store of value' as holdings hit 4.87 million tokens
crypto-web3.summary: Bitmine has amassed 4.87 million Ethereum tokens, representing 4.04% of all ether in circulation, while generating $212 million in annualized staking revenue. The company's substantial ETH position and positioning of Ethereum as a "wartime store of value" reflects institutional confidence in the network's long-term economic model.

crypto-web3.title: Coinone hit with $3.5 million fine, three-month partial suspension over AML violations: reports
crypto-web3.summary: South Korean cryptocurrency exchange Coinone has been fined $3.5 million and temporarily suspended for three months due to anti-money laundering compliance violations. The regulatory enforcement action underscores ongoing global scrutiny of crypto platforms' financial crime prevention measures.

crypto-web3.title: WLFI mints $25 million in fresh USD1 and burns $3 million, days after repayment claim
crypto-web3.summary: DeFi platform WLFI has minted $25 million in fresh USD1 stablecoin while simultaneously burning $3 million, just days after announcing a $25 million repayment of borrowed funds that had left depositors unable to withdraw. The concurrent minting and burning activity raises questions about the platform's financial management and stablecoin mechanics.

crypto-web3.title: There's a Way to Make Bitcoin Safe From Quantum Without a Fork, Researchers Say
crypto-web3.summary: Researchers have proposed a transaction design methodology that would protect Bitcoin users from future quantum computing threats without requiring a contentious network fork. The approach leverages existing Bitcoin protocol rules to enable quantum-resistant transactions, addressing a long-standing security concern in the cryptocurrency community.

crypto-web3.title: Circle CEO Allaire defends decision not to freeze USDC in Drift exploit, citing 'moral quandary'
crypto-web3.summary: Circle CEO Jeremy Allaire has defended the company's decision not to freeze USDC stablecoins following the Drift protocol exploit, asserting that Circle has clear legal and performance obligations to maintain normal operations. The stance reflects the tension between protecting users and maintaining stablecoin functionality during security incidents.

crypto-web3.title: 'Not Going to Stop at Bitcoin': Morgan Stanley Weighs Tokenization, Tax Solutions in Crypto Push
crypto-web3.summary: Morgan Stanley's crypto strategy is expanding beyond Bitcoin to include tokenization services and cryptocurrency tax solutions, signaling Wall Street's broadening engagement with digital assets. The bank's multi-pronged approach suggests sustained institutional adoption of blockchain technology across financial services.

startups-vc.title: North America Q1 Funding Surges Across Stages To Record Level
startups-vc.summary: U.S. and Canadian companies secured $252.6 billion in seed through growth-stage funding in Q1 2026, more than three times the prior quarter and the largest quarterly total on record. This represents a significant surge in venture capital activity across all funding stages in North America.

startups-vc.title: The Big 3 IPOs Will Dwarf Everything Else Since 2000. Combined.
startups-vc.summary: SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic are positioned to deliver IPOs that would collectively create more value than all VC-backed IPOs since 2000 combined, fundamentally reshaping the venture capital landscape. OpenAI has already closed a $122 billion funding round, while SpaceX filed confidentially with the SEC and Anthropic targets an October listing.

startups-vc.title: This Is A Momentous Year For Early-Stage Unicorns
startups-vc.summary: Forty-seven seed and early-stage companies achieved unicorn status in Q1 2026 alone, putting the year on track to deliver the largest cohort of young unicorns to date. This surge in early-stage exits reflects a robust and accelerating venture ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: China Leads Asia's Startup Funding To Its Highest Level In More Than 3 Years
startups-vc.summary: Asian startups received $27.4 billion across seed through growth-stage financings in Q1 2026, up approximately 20% from the prior quarter and nearly double year-ago levels, marking the highest quarterly total in more than three years. China led the funding surge, driving robust capital deployment across the region.

startups-vc.title: Slate Auto raises $650M to fund its affordable EV truck plans
startups-vc.summary: Slate Auto secured $650 million in its latest funding round, led by existing investor TWG Global, a firm backed by LA Dodgers owner Mark Walter, to advance its affordable electric vehicle truck development. The significant capital raise underscores investor confidence in the EV manufacturing segment.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Largest Financings Went To Defense, Wearables, Energy And Security
startups-vc.summary: Startup investors continued robust dealmaking activity this week with Austin-based Saronic, developer of autonomous vessels, leading with a $1.75 billion Series D funding round. Large financing rounds were distributed across defense, wearables, energy, and security sectors.

startups-vc.title: Databricks: Only 19% of Organizations Have Deployed AI Agents. But They're Already Creating 97% of Databases.
startups-vc.summary: According to Databricks' State of AI Agents 2026 report analyzing 20,000+ organizations, only 19% have deployed AI agents despite their outsized impact on database creation. The findings highlight a significant gap between AI agent adoption rates and their operational importance across enterprises.

startups-vc.title: Fintech Startups Globally Raise More Money In Far Fewer Deals In Q1 2026
startups-vc.summary: Global fintech startups raised $12 billion across 751 deals in the first quarter of 2026, representing a 5% increase in capital despite a 31% decline in deal volume compared to the same period in 2025. The trend indicates consolidation and larger average deal sizes in the fintech sector.

startups-vc.title: Sierra's Bret Taylor says the era of clicking buttons is over
startups-vc.summary: Sierra launched Ghostwriter, an agent-as-a-service tool designed to build other AI agents and replace traditional click-based web applications with natural language interfaces. Users can describe their needs in plain language, and Ghostwriter autonomously creates and deploys specialized agents to execute tasks.

startups-vc.title: What founders can learn from Anjuna's layoffs and recovery
startups-vc.summary: Anjuna Security, a venture-backed cybersecurity company, experienced rapid scaling to 75 employees in 2021 before facing significant challenges in 2022, offering lessons for founders on managing hypergrowth and market dynamics. The company's experience illustrates the risks of aggressive expansion without corresponding market validation.

asia-pacific.title: China says open Strait of Hormuz crucial for global interests as Trump blockade looms
asia-pacific.summary: China has called for a ceasefire in the Iran conflict to maintain open passage through the Strait of Hormuz, emphasizing its critical importance for international trade and energy supplies. The statement comes as the Trump administration announced a naval blockade targeting Iranian ports, a move with significant implications for global energy markets and Asia-Pacific trade routes.

asia-pacific.title: Taiwan industries cheer Beijing's 10-point plan to boost cross-strait ties
asia-pacific.summary: Taiwan's business groups and opposition party have welcomed Beijing's package of ten measures aimed at promoting economic and livelihood exchanges across the Taiwan Strait. The initiatives focus on economic cooperation rather than politically sensitive issues, offering potential relief to local industries facing recent pressures.

asia-pacific.title: US blockade of Strait of Hormuz is 'easy enough for them to do' but at what cost?
asia-pacific.summary: Analysts warn that while a U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz could provide strategic advantage against Iran, it risks destabilizing the regional ceasefire and deepening the global energy crisis. The move represents a significant escalation in Middle Eastern tensions with cascading effects on Asia-Pacific energy security.

asia-pacific.title: What Pakistani jets in Saudi Arabia mean for Gulf security in Iran war
asia-pacific.summary: Pakistan's deployment of an air force contingent to a Saudi Arabian Gulf coast base, concurrent with its mediation efforts between Iran and the United States, signals a potential deterrent to Iranian military escalation. The Pakistani presence introduces a new dimension to Gulf security dynamics while emphasizing Islamabad's role as a regional peace broker.

asia-pacific.title: South Korean president's Holocaust remarks spark Israel outcry
asia-pacific.summary: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung has drawn diplomatic criticism from Israel and domestic backlash after comparing Israeli military operations against Palestinians to the Holocaust. The controversy highlights tensions in Seoul's diplomatic approach to the Middle East conflict and its implications for international relations.

asia-pacific.title: Why Beijing isn't panicking over Hungarian PM Viktor Orban's stunning election defeat
asia-pacific.summary: China views Hungary's political transition following the centre-right Tisza Party's landslide election victory and Peter Magyar's ascension to prime minister as unlikely to significantly disrupt Beijing-Budapest relations. Chinese analysts suggest the change will have limited impact on China-EU relations overall, indicating confidence in maintaining strategic ties beyond individual leadership transitions.

asia-pacific.title: Australian PM to travel to Brunei and Malaysia to secure energy, fertilizer supplies
asia-pacific.summary: Australia's Prime Minister is conducting diplomatic visits to Brunei and Malaysia to secure critical supplies of energy and fertilizers, building on recent fuel and gas agreements with Singapore. The regional energy diplomacy reflects efforts to secure stable supply chains amid global market uncertainties.

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong to slash over 10,000 civil service posts by mid-2027: minister
asia-pacific.summary: Hong Kong's government plans to eliminate over 10,000 civil service positions by June 2027, with approximately 60 percent representing junior-level roles and 3 percent senior positions. The restructuring, announced by Civil Service Minister Ingrid Yeung, aims to optimize the workforce composition by reducing frontline staff.

asia-pacific.title: Uncertainty looms as US sanctions waiver for India expires
asia-pacific.summary: The expiration of a U.S. sanctions waiver for India amid geopolitical uncertainties in the Gulf region creates opportunities for India and Russia to strengthen bilateral ties. The development signals potential shifts in New Delhi's strategic alignments given energy security concerns and great power competition.

asia-pacific.title: Indonesia's Disinformation Bill and the Politics of 'Foreign Propaganda'
asia-pacific.summary: President Prabowo's administration in Indonesia has introduced legislation targeting disinformation and foreign propaganda, granting authorities significant powers in regulating information flows. The bill raises concerns among observers regarding potential impacts on press freedom and political discourse in the region's largest democracy.

energy-climate.title: Iran war analysis: How 60 nations have responded to the global energy crisis
energy-climate.summary: At least 60 countries have responded to the geopolitical disruption caused by conflict between the US and Israel and Iran, which has created a significant global energy crisis. The analysis examines international policy responses and energy market implications stemming from this major geopolitical event.

energy-climate.title: DeBriefed 10 April 2026: Worst energy crisis 'ever' | India withdraws COP33 bid | Drag artists and climate change
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's weekly digest covers the week's major climate and energy developments, including reports of an unprecedented global energy crisis and India's withdrawal from hosting COP33. The digest synthesizes key policy, market, and climate-related news for stakeholders.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: Record wind and solar saved UK from gas imports worth £1bn in March 2026
energy-climate.summary: Record renewable energy generation from wind and solar in March 2026 eliminated the need for approximately £1 billion in gas imports for the United Kingdom. The analysis demonstrates the economic and energy security benefits of high renewable penetration in the grid.

energy-climate.title: Marine heatwaves 'nearly double' the economic damage caused by tropical cyclones
energy-climate.summary: Research shows that tropical cyclones intensifying over marine heatwaves can become significantly more damaging, with marine heatwaves nearly doubling the economic impact of cyclone events. The findings highlight the compounding climate risks posed by ocean warming and extreme weather interaction.

energy-climate.title: India aims for 60% non-fossil power by 2035
energy-climate.summary: India has announced an ambitious target to achieve 60% non-fossil fuel electricity generation by 2035, supported by plans to install 500 GW of renewable energy capacity by 2030. The commitment represents a significant escalation of India's climate and energy transition goals.

energy-climate.title: IESO signs 20-year contracts for 14 renewable energy projects
energy-climate.summary: Ontario's Independent Electricity System Operator has secured 20-year power purchase agreements with 14 renewable energy projects expected to deliver over 3 TWh of annual electricity supply. The procurement represents a major step in building long-term renewable energy infrastructure in the province.

energy-climate.title: Fortescue fast-tracks delivery of 'world first' off-grid project combining solar, wind, up to 5 GWh of BESS
energy-climate.summary: Fortescue is accelerating deployment of a groundbreaking off-grid renewable energy project integrating solar, wind, and up to 5 GWh of battery energy storage to eliminate diesel use in iron ore mining operations. The project represents a significant industrial decarbonization initiative in the mining sector.

energy-climate.title: Jupiter, AMPIN open 1.3 GW solar factory in India
energy-climate.summary: Jupiter International and AMPIN Energy Transition have inaugurated a 1.3 GW solar cell and module manufacturing facility in Bhubaneswar, India, expanding domestic solar production capacity. The facility supports India's renewable energy expansion and manufacturing ambitions.

energy-climate.title: South Korea sets 20% renewable power goal for 2030
energy-climate.summary: South Korea has established a target to increase renewable energy's share of electricity generation to at least 20% by 2030. The goal reflects the country's commitment to accelerating its energy transition away from fossil fuels.

energy-climate.title: Managing cyber risks in the era of decentralized energy
energy-climate.summary: The transition toward decentralized energy resources creates new cybersecurity challenges requiring comprehensive protective measures alongside the resilience and flexibility benefits these systems provide. Industry experts highlight the need for proactive security frameworks in distributed energy networks.
